title: As a developer, I want a design system configured so that all UI components have consistent visual styling

acceptance_criteria.given: the frontend project is set up
acceptance_criteria.then: a custom colour palette is defined with primary, secondary, success, warning, error, and neutral tokens available as Tailwind classes

acceptance_criteria.when: a developer imports a shadcn component
acceptance_criteria.then: the component renders with the project colour tokens applied correctly

acceptance_criteria.when: any page is viewed on a mobile device (375px)
acceptance_criteria.then: the layout is fully responsive and no horizontal scrollbar appears

acceptance_criteria.when: an axe-core accessibility scan runs on any page
acceptance_criteria.then: colour contrast ratios meet WCAG AA — at least 4.5:1 for normal text

non_functional_requirements.accessibility: All colour tokens meet WCAG AA contrast ratios
non_functional_requirements.security: No external font or icon CDN calls — self-hosted or bundled only

definition_of_done: tailwind.config.js has custom colour palette defined
shadcn/ui initialised and at least Button, Card, Input, Badge components available
globals.css has CSS custom properties matching Tailwind tokens
Colour contrast verified in accessibility test
agent_instructions: Configure Tailwind with a warm neutral palette suitable for ecommerce. Primary: a trustworthy blue or indigo. Use shadcn/ui new-york style. Define CSS custom properties in globals.css so tokens are available both in Tailwind classes and inline styles. Do NOT use external CDN links for fonts — use next/font or vite-plugin-fonts.

title: As a shopper, I want a consistent page layout with navigation so that I can move around the site easily

acceptance_criteria.then: they see a header with the shop logo, navigation links (Home, Catalogue, My Orders), a cart icon showing item count, and a Sign In or user name depending on auth state

acceptance_criteria.given: a shopper is on a mobile device (375px wide)
acceptance_criteria.then: a mobile navigation drawer opens showing all nav links and closes when a link is tapped
they see a footer with copyright notice and links to Terms and Privacy
a loading indicator appears and disappears cleanly — no flash of unstyled content
there are 0 accessibility violations — nav has role=navigation, landmarks are correct

non_functional_requirements.performance: Layout shell renders in under 100ms — no layout shift
non_functional_requirements.accessibility: Skip to main content link present. Nav has aria-label. Mobile menu is keyboard accessible.
non_functional_requirements.security: No authentication tokens exposed in the DOM

definition_of_done: Header component with logo, nav, cart icon, auth state
Mobile nav drawer with hamburger trigger
agent_instructions: Header should be sticky. Cart icon shows a badge with item count from cart context (wire to CartContext from STORY-005 or use placeholder count of 0 if not yet available). Use shadcn/ui Sheet for the mobile drawer. The RootLayout component wraps all page-level routes.

title: As a shopper, I want clear loading and error states throughout the app so that I always know what is happening

acceptance_criteria.given: any page is loading data from the API
acceptance_criteria.then: a skeleton loader matching the shape of the content appears — not a spinning circle
a user-friendly error message appears with a Retry button — no raw error text or stack traces shown to the user

acceptance_criteria.given: a user action succeeds (add to cart, register, login)
acceptance_criteria.then: a toast notification appears in the top right for 3 seconds confirming success

acceptance_criteria.given: a user action fails (network error, validation error)
acceptance_criteria.then: a toast notification appears with a red background and descriptive error message

acceptance_criteria.given: a page has no data to show (empty orders list, no search results)
acceptance_criteria.then: an illustrated empty state with a helpful message and a relevant CTA button appears

non_functional_requirements.performance: Skeleton loaders appear within 16ms of request start — no visible delay before skeleton
non_functional_requirements.accessibility: Toast notifications announced to screen readers via aria-live. Skeleton loaders have aria-busy=true.
non_functional_requirements.security: Error messages never expose internal error details, stack traces, or server paths

definition_of_done: SkeletonCard and SkeletonList components match product card and list shapes
Toast notification system using shadcn/ui Toaster
EmptyState component with icon, message, and CTA
agent_instructions: Use shadcn/ui Skeleton for loading states. Use shadcn/ui Toaster + toast() for notifications. Skeleton shapes must match the real content — ProductCard skeleton should be the same dimensions as the real ProductCard. EmptyState should accept icon, title, description, and ctaLabel + ctaHref props.
